response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software craftsmanship and 20 years of development experience, this role requires deep C++ expertise and industrial automation domain knowledge. The candidate has only ~6 months of C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014–2015) and their entire career is rooted in web, SaaS, and media development—not embedded systems, manufacturing automation, or industrial cybersecurity. For a mid-senior specialist position, the technical and domain gaps are too significant to bridge on the job.
strengths: Extensive 20-year software development track record with demonstrated ability to master multiple languages and architectures
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship, testing practices (TDD), and mentoring—directly aligned with role's quality and team-building expectations
Full-stack and full-domain breadth (web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure) showing adaptability and learning agility
concerns: Severely limited C++ expertise: only ~6 months of professional C++ experience in 2014–2015; current primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript
No industrial, manufacturing, or automation domain background; entire career in web, SaaS, media, and fintech—completely different problem space from on-premises industrial solutions

No evidence of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices, or embedded-systems security; education is in Computer Science, not Engineering (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech)

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / manufacturing domain experience
Strong software craftsmanship and agile practices
